title: load_chats: no fallback if server rejects ids:[] in Chat/get
description: load_chats calls chat_get(Some(&[])) when chat_query returns no IDs, serializing to {"ids":[]}. RFC 8620 §5.1 permits ids:[] (returns empty list plus state), but some JMAP server implementations reject an empty ids array as an invalid argument rather than returning an empty list with a state token. If the server rejects ids:[], load_chats propagates the ClientError, chat_state remains None, and every subsequent SSE StateChange triggers a full reload via chat_delta_sync (which calls load_chats again, hitting the same rejection loop). The code has no fallback to calling chat_get(None) (fetch-all) when the empty-ids call fails. The load_chats doc comment explains the intent but does not document this server-rejection risk or the absence of a fallback. Fix: either add a fallback to chat_get(None) on error, or document the assumption explicitly with a note that known-good servers (kith) accept ids:[] and that the absence of a fallback is intentional for those targets.
